Now we are taking our winning formula national. With Democrats stretched thin by House and Senate races, defending democracy in the courts will not happen unless we do it. ==? | | == | | | | We are counting on you. And right now, the country is too. In solidarity, Team Defend Our Courts | | == | Defend Our Courts | P.O. Box 45569 | Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | | | |

body:
It's worse than you think, Peter.

| | | [](https://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=7273260329054a15a955652ad49083e6&_e=RbC47KqQLgykThK77qd75Ka7nMOJ-30fRPTKWvOidrrMxgTesyKbm021CwpEI6INKI1mZtaKDR03SjTnnqAZmsbRHV00z8An5fgKlCZhYRSi6-zv7PLQDWMxTaIBgXVNEQDIz_UxR1RO8t21qsAeOuoFotZPR7WprDnPuTbeaqCREcjjQK3yBX8c7g_JkgPH00I-DrBm_TpOQHgoinO7mJ92Fh0IAR6M7GLkeZUfUiejSFTqzKzVbnHd3_EGZbuDyj8WwhXmnqYP35owUS4IepmKPwmUeZPRU_FEvOEgniVJ_wZq1ahQWhsp617LN2foGnx4ONKrFdvTAnNK4ot8W6cVM3GrikFNwEqus4cdoTTkdgQcwM7ug1CCnaknlNQE3HdEZFUCpyPIOzr1fHVrw49_8llj2fykADusfSXNwjKJP2uhbooQbwQZ9YAYceqJXD_EwEYYyZqUGpW2Pe8PSXc3Bj465QfEfk_3eyh4uIbBVVqlCqoNxy6dAdgsfVXKws0mrlaNgYqIu_1di7j7BpgudER4_AI9KXgJoFUsOvMaWw8QUf1dzeEUgIwFLdQ5Pi1wq3-6t2CzIl1hyDoyf_p2zxnrE6-PnPww14thX5C7pDjMwTcGEa81VRU2SIGxycTo82VTudl39Ma3gw0VNOXtLf-vThUJ-zeaLSEhc-xzjOCM8ySrUq39SLn5IJM7QyCxr2ypnivHYqEQ-bh8rs9JYlAX5XqMGpZHd9qF6SqshBwwcFrvevy63HwxEUA2hE2qTqoRMSBUb4RSQPn_qswZIt2bJgoc89PYNXpMEHd4NIfwJSv06KQaZ_zwwsmtE9YdMPx9eX8MUUkCTM8-Yi6gV3-dIlnJO739dbvGt2Q%3D)
---
| | Peter,

The truth is ugly, but I won’t sugarcoat it:

The Democrat establishment in Washington right now couldn’t care less about families like yours and mine. They answer to coastal elites, global bureaucrats, and radical activists — not to the American people.

They mock our faith. They try to shame our values. They chip away at our freedoms while living by a completely different set of rules. __**[_Not convinced? See the facts for yourself:_](https://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=7273260329054a15a955652ad49083e6&_e=RbC47KqQLgykThK77qd75Ka7nMOJ-30fRPTKWvOidrrMxgTesyKbm021CwpEI6INKI1mZtaKDR03SjTnnqAZmsbRHV00z8An5fgKlCZhYRSi6-zv7PLQDWMxTaIBgXVNEQDIz_UxR1RO8t21qsAeOuoFotZPR7WprDnPuTbeaqCREcjjQK3yBX8c7g_JkgPH00I-DrBm_TpOQHgoinO7mJ92Fh0IAR6M7GLkeZUfUiejSFTqzKzVbnHd3_EGZbuDyj8WwhXmnqYP35owUS4IepmKPwmUeZPRU_FEvOEgniVJ_wZq1ahQWhsp617LN2fovEOQE5ktq9bbkp-jArxbvhg1zJvNNrYeEMDbuhLZXse7A3OST2QBw7pyiHoEeZLpyrnPLrwQ8QyLhpUsRP0v8cypf1akeBNWfNGLPRyyJo5cQ5Dm1OTM4LMOgty94lHxT8l0gD3T4KspRLp8Pirzatni2UMk_fGUPmWissKzJzIhh-hJsQS21XqKR8Cfl3EokbTuw_UQfqqGxb2TrWHA45Ll1wN0vlY1u2I8yHpSY9ik919AGK532ICgReswsQSLz0ug1B2yistQbQHXhPiZG51rYCVsy6SoLq0Ygo2UqYy4igXHi53JwvrKOWAUrTSm4FlYzJHV8twguAGiM3bFblVirvPWyrVR7XvZTC8GZIgF23drRNAc_ViypHt1-houzJRAs4V8nLvorKZqTiZFgzs7sxhMaYXwtFeShO1qGYVF04caPTb9AdgTbEPY9dQJBtV8oMwLcGqstTuTd_LrJsZUsXKlWLWCb3xTzaV1hcUTLPianx67U9kw_jNNUZcAqtMZpaPH_SaLPHyzvNIeA9S0GYbBPkl5O4Lcw5ax1Gc%3D)**
---
| | DEMOCRAT PLAYBOOK
---
| | 1\. They fly private while telling working families to “cut back.” 2\. They back open borders and endless crime while hiding behind walls and armed security. 3\. They rig the economy against those who work hard, rewarding those who don’t. 4\. They hijack real suffering just to score cheap political points. 5\. And they’re more loyal to their special interest donors than to the Constitution they swore to uphold.
---
| | _These people aren’t public servants. _

_They’re _parasites_ feeding off the system while working Americans suffer. _
---
They’ve sold out this country to the highest bidder and laughed in our faces. This isn’t just corruption. **It’s an all-out attack on _everything_ we believe in.**

The Left is furious after their humiliating 2024 losses and are desperate to take back power in 2026. They want to flip the House, retake the Senate, and push their radical agenda on hardworking Americans again.

The Democrats are funded by billionaires who don’t care about you. Me? I’m fighting with real Americans like you, Peter. Small-dollar donations from patriots like you keep us in this fight. Without you, the Left wins.
---
[Make no mistake, even $1 can decide if we keep control or let the radical Left take over. Chip in now to stop them dead in their tracks:](https://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=7273260329054a15a955652ad49083e6&_e=RbC47KqQLgykThK77qd75Ka7nMOJ-30fRPTKWvOidrrMxgTesyKbm021CwpEI6INKI1mZtaKDR03SjTnnqAZmsbRHV00z8An5fgKlCZhYRSi6-zv7PLQDWMxTaIBgXVNEQDIz_UxR1RO8t21qsAeOuoFotZPR7WprDnPuTbeaqCREcjjQK3yBX8c7g_JkgPH00I-DrBm_TpOQHgoinO7mJ92Fh0IAR6M7GLkeZUfUiejSFTqzKzVbnHd3_EGZbuDyj8WwhXmnqYP35owUS4IepmKPwmUeZPRU_FEvOEgniVJ_wZq1ahQWhsp617LN2foGgNOMvkMnRjUKFX8JswaqQ8gZxcFaZFyqoulCdPxr9vH8GJHilE-vtpjmvdK4qgg056ryDvFAn80GRAZ3gztZgemiWZdimZnzlUkx6cKidspMxKiRi3LaPqpSTbvoJPFQtRDyTCPJ_tVoHZrKYMHA2hh22UYIEtJpnvsRObJJXBZe6ipHSyooEwUlItiie-XLvz8fVYICkTDPYYyHIi733Ii1COvuPImMHK3KhTZYoi-VW3_Oih_A5544eKl7pCSROFu1feI6hjjxxkKu7fQLxk4CD5x9vdZvJ_ujW7by3x-jr_DcI6TQBs3QxZDFhTMYxCzgh5_hGUVmR9TZqdCsNRKZNoAZT97m9kKvAGtnw-CZsM2nF27jPpsunskcG3rQqOdQFurZ8s9240FlhmZnOHjmFwGdLIqYaV5FNJdWZt09Zs4MGf65evrrFinoPxqqgIibN3cGkIKLuYshosp8zhLYy5TVBIWtvmGBoucZxpFCmEuNyQ8DWU6g1tmJMZRsJ_kue7azg9IMrd2UNJq5teGQ50ygJF-dOJKbn2e_jQ%3D)
---
We’re in charge now. But if we don’t fight hard and deliver results, 2026 will hand the Senate and House back to the Democrats. \- Senator Katie Britt
---
[](https://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=7273260329054a15a955652ad49083e6&_e=RbC47KqQLgykThK77qd75Ka7nMOJ-30fRPTKWvOidrrMxgTesyKbm021CwpEI6INHQF7HrEcxaEh8ZGGaLO7MucsoXoCILHM5bAN5-J9qqErSofSGI0Da9wEKym913XCLgEUPsXlrxl9WBMoeAqdkHM-E_R7_6rv5Z8CvbVusOvbHlown5I4lzK6-_7tlFb7Xi3xCRFM3fDW-Iexqi8FMb8dXBypHMcELAzWi8FOsIGhz-U0Ly41lZFGcLSNxLV5n-RMZJTmRHBpP5iKwCnDG6vtpSEZ_QOkmoVIe1kdUsJ21cAG0kQXCP4df2Z3cUJrbq55BNP3-p1gCW5IvTguSb_D_DxS0ojQrNVBl3Gscq3Pg7DHTbNSi2XivkA_zxFAuOlEVsc07YeAhl5vmZhlkTsQ9yG39mZHtPDnHwGVCWUDXcavL20pPEecZ_C0nrzHkrX9FAtadYYrfGlgfQNaA841vtM6kU7CKcTl1tgfwDQ%3D)
---
I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing _everything_ they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?
---
[ STAND WITH KATIE >> ](https://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=7273260329054a15a955652ad49083e6&_e=RbC47KqQLgykThK77qd75Ka7nMOJ-30fRPTKWvOidrrMxgTesyKbm021CwpEI6INHQF7HrEcxaEh8ZGGaLO7MucsoXoCILHM5bAN5-J9qqErSofSGI0Da9wEKym913XCslhlvzQe2h3imt_QTl6-Ow_HE0gE07gt30CzizBdeB0sE3G4-NfKoaEbNU281InjQcwPi-aaN6rFzi0atX0A4kMStJwCgT0kZl9n1L-R5leiQziapchKFLUzIrNX_h2Db2hO8rPV54TKV5_1i_GoaqvbpmHte2nVDnpRHauSCRwzeTY-567HCRE2hpt_gT__1IzrBtvR6T2nkrSN-hfte4QsYcxb_PgUGpGL3WtbPr1tX0MNj7_8_qgM133ov69ztzciRxAZjbXtndCrnNja6cpijFvaLD5_dWxsMXza_nPNC68-SXZZyoDRof6vgLlpC75Jpww7zDyzI8or-XV7QL8UG6L5yIkOpoKJruSsAIY%3D)

subject: We have a new family member!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christina for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
Hi there,
Join us in welcoming our new puppy, Aretha Franklin Elizabeth Hines, to the family! Life in America these days is often chaotic, exhausting, and uncertain—layered with big national headlines and the personal struggles we all carry—which is probably why this felt like exactly the right moment to share something joyful.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
We have missed our pup Stevie Wonder dearly, and after a lot of conversations, we decided it was time to open our home to a dog who needed one.
We adopted through I HeartDogs Rescue [https://www.iheartdogs.org/] , an amazing organization in Warren dedicated to giving dogs a second chance and helping families find lifelong companions.
And let me tell you, Miss Aretha doesn’t suffer fools. In the short time we’ve been a family, she’s taught us a lot about RESPECT and being rock steady.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs] https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
You will see little Miss Aretha out on the campaign trail with us, greeting our neighbors at events, and keeping me on message—she has a great ear for what resonates!
If you’d like to help power this campaign and keep Aretha and our campaign staffers stocked with megaphones and yard signs, will you chip in today? Every dollar helps us reach more voters, knock more doors, and build the kind of community Stevie helped build, and Aretha is already proud to protect. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
CHIP IN TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
Let’s do this, united together. 🐾
Christina and Aretha Hines
P.S. If you’ve ever adopted a rescue animal, hit reply and tell me your story. I’d love to hear it!
If you believe in building kinder communities, please share this email, volunteer, or chip in to help us keep growing this movement. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
CHIP IN TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs]
VOLUNTEER WITH US [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdZei-gGVwkYb3UfD9zl3KNwyGpDLIlc07FBfQIqDyw9HBh-g/viewform]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260308_ArethaIntro_actrs] Christina is a lifelong advocate for justice and a former prosecutor for survivors of sexual assault, child abuse, and human trafficking.
Now she’s running to bring that same dedication to Congress, working to put the middle class first, protect the American Dream, and ensure every community can thrive. She fights for safe neighborhoods, fair opportunities, and a future where everyone can succeed.
To learn more about Christina, visit ChristinaHinesForCongress.com [christinahinesforcongress.com] .

clean_body: My father and I couldn’t do this without patriots like you. Open this note of gratitude. | Fellow American, it's Don Jr. I wanted to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude for your unwavering support. My father and I know that this fight isn't possible without patriots like you who are willing to stand up for what's right. We're honored to have you on our team, Fellow American. ## Runoff Candidate Forum
**On the Agenda:** "**Go Texan** " program for Texas based businesses that drive national and international customers for your company with **Tony Noun** **Run Off candidates** in the primary that have confirmed: **Warren Howell** for County Judge **Orlando Sanchez** for County Judge **Todd Frankfort** for 1st Court of Appeals Place 3 **James Wright** Railroad Commissioner **Alexander Hale** for CD7 US Congress **Cindy Siegel** HCRP chairman -**Election Wrap Up Report with Stan Stanart**
## "American Honor Fund"
Building Back AMERICA partnership with BIZPAC business accelerator BIZPAC.us PITCH ROOM Business Accelerator is launching full force after a successful pilot year 2025 **Where: Tanglewood Ballroom 5430 Westheimer Ct.** **When: March 12th 11am to 1pm** **Dress: Business Sharp** _We're changing up the menu for better food choices...so, RSVP now_ (**LOWER PRICE FOR LUNCH and BETTER FOOD CHOICES)**

name: Team Virginians For Fair Elections
subject: The battle over redistricting nationwide
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Virginians for Fair Elections
body:
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/629?refcode=em20260308-292&t=1&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
[](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260308_1.rQX69v&amount=3&refcode=20260308-292&t=2&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
**FIRST:** Texas Republicans pushed through a new map that could add up to five GOP seats.
**THEN:** The Supreme Court allowed it. Missouri, Ohio, North Carolina, and Florida are moving in the same direction. _This is not subtle._ It’s a coordinated effort to reshape the national landscape before voters even cast their ballots.
**NOW:** Virginia is in the middle of it.
Unlike Texas or Florida, Virginia lawmakers chose a different path. Instead of backroom deals, they put a constitutional amendment on the ballot so voters can decide. The Virginia Supreme Court has cleared the way. Now it is up to the people.
But here is the reality: if voters do not understand what is at stake, if misinformation fills the vacuum, if turnout is low in this special election, the opportunity to level the playing field could slip away.
_And early voting began just two days ago._
Virginians for Fair Elections is racing against the clock right now to:
* Educate voters about what this amendment actually does
* Push back against confusion and partisan spin
* Reach as many voters as possible before April 21
* Turn out supporters now that early voting has officially started

We don’t have months, we have just weeks until April 21._And beginning this past Friday, ballots are being cast._ [**Can you rush a donation of $3 or more to help fuel our efforts?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260308_1.rQX69v&amount=3&refcode=20260308-292&t=3&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
**[Donate $3 ››](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260308_1.rQX69v&amount=3&refcode=20260308-292&t=22&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)**
If you believe election outcomes should be earned, not engineered, now is the moment to act, [**can you rush a contribution today to help power our early vote push now that polls are open?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260308_1.rQX69v&amount=3&refcode=20260308-292&t=23&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
Every dollar right now goes directly toward voter outreach during the most critical stretch of this campaign.
[**Let’s meet this moment. Can you chip in anything you can right now?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260308_1.rQX69v&amount=3&refcode=20260308-292&t=24&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
— Team Virginians for Fair Elections

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_vafe_2026_footer?refcode=em&t=25&refcode2=292_12592_9sjBRA&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=26&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/update-contact-information/?&t=27&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)
* [Unsubscribe](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=292%2E12592%2E9sjBRA)

Contributions or gifts to Virginians for Fair Elections are not tax deductible.

name: Redistricting Action HQ
subject: With you, we’ll keep moving forward
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
We can’t afford to fall back on democracy — help strengthen our work in the fights ahead.
|
---

Peter, the clocks have moved forward — and so must we.
Thanks to you and this powerful grassroots community, our fair maps movement is driving toward a better, brighter future. And we’re doing it despite all of the unrelenting attempts to undermine representative democracy today.
To give you a sense of our impact, **more than 207,000 individuals** have put their support behind our fight. **We’ve taken over 396,000 actions** — calling, emailing, and petitioning to end attacks on power grabs and defend our democracy.
We’re so grateful for your generosity and willingness to act. But we all know that our work is far from over. **Right now, anti-democracy forces across the country are trying to unfairly manipulate the maps and deny our power.**
[**Donate now to power our response and ensure that the will of the people prevails in the face of impending mid-decade redraws and extreme gerrymanders.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126904394/606331004/-1482690219?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_MovingForward_email_20260308-AOTL-Tier1-MovingForward_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260308-AOTL-Tier1-MovingForward_&utm_campaign=MovingForward&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY3ZTYwMTFiLWY3MWEtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTFmMGE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WCNPut2ZvbCO3tGmo1kELFAcTX_9B-eBi-jCjBdG5R4=)
Your support is critically important as we must double down on our efforts to stop power grabs across the country, especially in states like:
* **Florida** , where legislators are pushing for a mid-decade redraw, despite strong opposition, and have passed their own version of the anti-voter SAVE America Act — both of which will make it harder for Floridians to have their voices heard.
* **Missouri** , where the state has gone to great lengths to defend a new, excessively gerrymandered map, even after it was challenged in multiple courts cases and after voters mobilized a referendum to override it. At the same time, state leaders are seeking to “redo” the 2020 Census and exclude all eligible voters.
* **Utah** , where politicians have been accused of handing in fraudulent signatures to undermine fair maps reform and put a measure to revive a blatantly partisan gerrymander on this November’s ballot.

Our fair maps movement has defended democracy in the face of attacks like these and others ongoing today. [**With your donation now, we can continue to move forward on our most critical efforts to fight back against schemes that aim to silence voters.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126904397/606331020/-1482690219?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_MovingForward_email_20260308-AOTL-Tier1-MovingForward_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260308-AOTL-Tier1-MovingForward_&utm_campaign=MovingForward&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY3ZTYwMTFiLWY3MWEtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTFmMGE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WCNPut2ZvbCO3tGmo1kELFAcTX_9B-eBi-jCjBdG5R4=)
Thank you,
The Redistricting Action Team

Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from Redistricting Action by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. And we’re doing it despite all of the unrelenting attempts to undermine representative democracy today. To give you a sense of our impact, more than 207,000 individuals have put their support behind our fight. We’ve taken over 396,000 actions — calling, emailing, and petitioning to end attacks on power grabs and defend our democracy. We’re so grateful for your generosity and willingness to act. But we all know that our work is far from over. Right now, anti-democracy forces across the country are trying to unfairly manipulate the maps and deny our power. = Your support is critically important as we must double down on our efforts to stop power grabs across the country, especially in states like: Florida , where legislators are pushing for a mid-decade redraw, despite strong opposition, and have passed their own version of the anti-voter SAVE America Act — both of which will make it harder for Floridians to have their voices heard. Missouri , where the state has gone to great lengths to defend a new, excessively gerrymandered map, even after it was challenged in multiple courts cases and after voters mobilized a referendum to override it. At the same time, state leaders are seeking to “redo” the 2020 Census and exclude all eligible voters. Utah , where politicians have been accused of handing in fraudulent signatures to undermine fair maps reform and put a measure to revive a blatantly partisan gerrymander on this November’s ballot. Our fair maps movement has defended democracy in the face of attacks like these and others ongoing today. = Thank you, The Redistricting Action Team Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from Redistricting Action by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. and | = ** | PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND. body:
Please don’t ignore House GOP’s 35X Impact opportunity?
Peter - It's Newt Gingrich.
I wish I was reaching out under better circumstances.
House GOP leaders have been reaching out over the last week seeking your support.
With everything on the line, [they even activated a 35X IMPACT opportunity.](https://links.trueamericanconservatives.com/s/c/PMh9gbGhjin9VaioKHdNmRef6cRj5lXhnLvd7XjUC1iOcqcJdtuGVgdQV-I3bZDFIF5ZN5VdavxQ1yOl_2ulTNbL5nEzxzNi3BHuQtx9ghbX1wURGa_tnQIiPAWdNKxGsbw5PjKkX9XtBtW_v4q4ir0BtyXmMCTVrvnqTFCsr0w4V3aUAzthoQ4wfgXpbi6H6ZoOzD4hVAIgsYl9w06fN6y-Ecsnk4-A4RwmBIHMS0JPPxiEMuFUntUpQh269z8-4or5LQfqtDdFZPXnqp7S9u79LiBeSQOupH1oJpV6iKpCFjlTVeFSQYEiUe-GAY_4Yli_BnU8RukSlhE5RGeM8Efx4O27awPegUGRu51ZdEGHjAOHrxw3Qwq4XWLb7VXJ3VNgTpSKPlENq4YJLfgx-HyCvw8r7OxffaAwJ_JXZBcRB8bIoV3ST6niJaTyLplKyG61eucAOX1WL7ttRYSXZIiObJYymESf4bR_PGFHXsanLWNzOPaiT6WmmbFRSfs_qIZx9YsxbME9yYY31gXvjRp3ggcQ5Mv7wktvQRTH3KooRJs3H0V80ZGb5P40YU9PLisIRkLuc7DYHTDdayiNqLsrNBmL2rfsaMQJQ_aCmtgrPWA1hdUJYKu0BYQdKAIJ9aFVW-Qzl8nVeVWpHnQVkWzBNd5-2axzGZ6ocbOk2aIXzRAWTZjhly4xUBhBqkMVmm7OezK-lmcgL_o78f70ALSt5g8IvnjRX_4B2eVZEGg6i2oHHjgnOewZs_jtTua_e1Oz/FZ_VExdX16xfafKrPk7QXl-eNF5t-rgB/18)

name: Charles Booker
subject: We’re leading the polls
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
But we can’t let up for a second.
| We’re not just ahead in this race, family — **_we’re GROWING our lead in the primary._** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/104409?t=6&akid=23206%2E878179%2EO7PV7s) I’m not the big money candidate. And I’m not the establishment pick. But people are making their voices heard all across Kentucky, it’s clear that this state is ready for real, meaningful change.

[I believe in us, and I know this team has what it takes to win. name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: Tell Me How This Ends...
body:
Winning the war in Iran may be easy; securing the peace will be more challenging.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..PsQXjG1Wu3JxZMEJr4jcpDSj9U2VYN6XsBz5bf6SYCE?) for more

# [Tell Me How This Ends...](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=190217238&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkwMjE3MjM4LCJpYXQiOjE3NzI5Nzg4MTIsImV4cCI6MTc3NTU3MDgxMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.moBuFNvcX9yGW2bfeXTwytAgtxl7jb9ywmGyhuMHjL0)
### Winning the war in Iran may be easy; securing the peace will be more challenging.
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Mar 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=190217238&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkwMjE3MjM4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3Mjk3ODgxMiwiZXhwIjoxNzc1NTcwODEyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r7SL3XK--50MwMK7G3Ey2SMAco95rSvjzE61EaFc8Nc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=190217238&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkwMjE3MjM4LCJpYXQiOjE3NzI5Nzg4MTIsImV4cCI6MTc3NTU3MDgxMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.moBuFNvcX9yGW2bfeXTwytAgtxl7jb9ywmGyhuMHjL0&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=190217238&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkwMjE3MjM4LCJpYXQiOjE3NzI5Nzg4MTIsImV4cCI6MTc3NTU3MDgxMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.moBuFNvcX9yGW2bfeXTwytAgtxl7jb9ywmGyhuMHjL0)
---
| | [](https://substack..XbDbDZIW0mhy6q9eTzMwQvbg3eH_5KhQdMlUYDhwbc0?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/tell-me-how-this-ends?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1b80fd34-255b-4e29-9c79-4f68ff714ed4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In March 2003, as the 101st Airborne rolled toward Baghdad, its commander--a two-star general named David Petraeus--turned to an embedded [Washington Post reporter](https://substack.com/redirect/aa3829b1-4479-4d2e-812f-4584f966efce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and asked for a glimpse into the future: _"**Tell me how this ends**." _It became the most famous quote of the Iraq War, and eerily prescient. We invaded, toppled Saddam, disbanded the army, purged the government, and then spent twenty years (and a few trillion dollars) discovering that winning the war was the easy part.
And now, as the United States and Israel decisively move to put the final nails in the Iranian regime's coffin, I can't help but ask: "Are we destined to make the same mistakes we did last time?" On the surface, it seems we've learned a great deal. We aren't pouring in troops or dumping in billions of dollars, and U.S. President Donald Trump doesn't appear to have much appetite for nation-building or democracy-promotion. He even seems reluctant when it comes to regime change, instead suggesting the people of Iran should take on that project.
And yet, I am now asking myself the same question Petraeus asked 20 years ago: **How is this going to end?**
So far, the military campaign seems to be progressing well, but it doesn't seem like much thought has been given to what follows. As former State Department official Gabe Noronha noted earlier this year, the United States is great at removing hostile leaders, but we're not as good at setting up the country for post-conflict governance.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/896c9d2b-6333-4ef8-a25c-b615ce1bda8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
It's been one week since the U.S. and Israel [launched Operation Epic Fury against Iran](https://substack.com/redirect/a7601100-7923-4c91-8589-55fc6f492318?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Khamenei is dead. Tehran is burning. [Over 1,300 Iranians have been killed](https://substack.com/redirect/48277215-b63b-40b1-809b-445bc4fe55ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Six American service members are gone. Already it appears that the rest of the region is being drawn into the conflict. In its last dying gasp, the regime has lashed out and attacked 11 countries and international shipping. (See the bottom of this post for my summary of the regional attacks).
These attacks have already spurred some of these countries to join in on the effort. If this conflict continues to spread - dragging in more countries, the EU, Turkey - the impact could be widespread and destabilizing. Remember the unintended consequences of the Arab Spring? The Muslim Brotherhood in charge of Egypt (for a time), Libya and Syria disintegrating into anarchy, a wave of refugees flooding Europe and spurring a populist backlash...the disintegration of Iran could be materially more problematic for the region.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Uofs9RPBIdvtR2KBumf2xSMx-TxxQxSJC_XPVQlbBTo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=190217238)
And let's not forget about the context in which this is happening... a global economy that's been battling inflation, coping with artificial intelligence disruption, trade and tariff wars, and currency confusion. In the midst of those cross-currents, Iran's strategy--[launching nearly a thousand drones and missiles at Gulf targets](https://substack.com/redirect/e72adfbd-50d7-4d26-984f-b5cbda017f9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - is de facto shutting down the Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint through which 20% or so of global oil travels.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dbeb11a6-4eed-41af-af46-7c7d0a1b30b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[https://www.visualcapitalist.com/charted-oil-trade-through-the-strait-of-hormuz-by-country/](https://substack.com/redirect/10f1c822-15e4-40fa-ae9a-e681e26e7784?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Maersk has suspended all Gulf operations](https://substack.com/redirect/f2be63ec-c975-4ffd-977d-3ce8b616e51e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [oil is over $90](https://substack.com/redirect/eeeea30f-4fde-4808-b489-bdc7d565723e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the Dow is dropping like a rock. It would be silly to suggest that this impact is not being contemplated by Republican and Democrat strategists developing their messaging for the 2026 midterm elections. Remember the [2006 midterms](https://substack.com/redirect/2152e38d-8467-4302-98e8-e19aa34f106a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)? If this drags into the summer, what is the impact going to be on the economy? Could elevated commodity prices drive inflation higher, leading the Fed to adopt a "wait and see" strategy rather than lowering rates? What would the impact of such a decision be on the US housing market? Is it conceivable we'll be facing a stagnant or contracting economy and inflation, in a repeat of the 1970s oil-shock driven stagflation?
The Iranian regime has been [decapitated](https://substack.com/redirect/7361f3b5-26b8-4278-bb22-395a9eab493e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and much of its leadership has been killed. Moreover, the intense bombing is taking out not only military targets but also intelligence and security sites. Who is going to be left to secure the country? Or run the country? This is the kinetic version of the de-Baathification that barred anyone with ties to Saddam's Baath Party from government, including from the military and police. Overnight, the people who knew how to keep the peace, keep the lights on, run the courts, manage the water supply, were all gone. What followed was anarchy, then insurgency, then civil war. Is Iran headed down a similar path?
Let's take a closer look. The IRGC isn't just a military force. It runs construction companies, banks, telecom networks, and import-export businesses. It is a state within a state. When you decapitate the Supreme Leader, kill key commanders, and destroy the IRGC's infrastructure, you haven't just removed a regime. You've removed **the operating system of the country.** Who is going to reboot what's left? Look at Libya and Syria to see how this could unfold.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/5a3e84b9-6c1c-486a-9eba-83a13724ed07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Indeed, who is going to run the country after the war ends is very much an open question. The [former Crown Prince](https://substack.com/redirect/142b7f30-a420-49dd-92bc-91d8979871e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is on the sideline, chomping at the bit to get into the game, but it is not clear how much support he has inside the country. One thing is clear: he certainly doesn't have an army. How he or anyone else would secure the country still very much remains to be seen. This reminds me very much of [Ahmed Chalabi](https://substack.com/redirect/0f929cc1-adbc-4ee0-99a6-df802af32e72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the Iraqi exile who was supposed to run a pro-Western, U.S.-back government after the war. Didn't exactly work out as hoped.
[Trump says he wants a role in choosing Iran's next leader](https://substack.com/redirect/f2be63ec-c975-4ffd-977d-3ce8b616e51e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and has called Mojtaba Khamenei "unacceptable." But the question isn't who leads Iran next. It's who runs the electricity grid, staffs the hospitals, and prevents the country from fracturing into armed fiefdoms. We didn't have that answer in Iraq. We don't have it in Iran.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Uofs9RPBIdvtR2KBumf2xSMx-TxxQxSJC_XPVQlbBTo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=190217238)
Any attempt to secure the country is going to involve boots on the ground, but it's clear that the administration doesn't want American feet in those boots. That's why the CIA is reportedly [arming Kurdish forces along the Iraq-Iran border](https://substack.com/redirect/856a8388-a3e9-494c-832c-de612d33ad2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), with [Mossad promising them an autonomous region in post-regime Iran](https://substack.com/redirect/73f82ea1-dc52-456e-87e7-f211c6babc81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But is it a good idea to use a minority group to overthrow an ethnic majority?
Indeed, this isn't the first time we've [used the Kurds](https://substack.com/redirect/106c77c1-6595-4fdc-84be-a5faf0b1f185?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). There was the first Iraq war, then the second, then Syria, to name a few. And after the war ends, the Kurds tend to be reluctant to give up their arms; just look at what's [happening in Syria](https://substack.com/redirect/324771bd-91f1-41ef-851a-bd584c84f536?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) now. Let's not forget that Iran is a multi-ethnic country; could this end with its balkanization? This move sure seems like a glidepath to civil war, similar to Iraq. Plus anything involving the Kurds and guns always sets their neighbors on edge. [Iraq has already warned it won't tolerate border incursions](https://substack.com/redirect/a6bc0398-5ea5-41e7-bbe0-a6fed1fc384e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Turkey is watching](https://substack.com/redirect/23e0e85b-e6e5-43b6-916e-132b8b7baa86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with its finger on the trigger. It certainly hasn't hesitated to [bomb the Kurds in the past](https://substack.com/redirect/507b50c1-3cd2-4d76-a42c-439ee40c4b3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
## [Pizza, Iran, and a Zen Master](https://substack.com/redirect/1c887da7-a308-470c-95d8-69518069fc9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| June 29, 2025
[](https://substack.com/redirect/1c887da7-a308-470c-95d8-69518069fc9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Last week I wrote about the Pentagon Pizza Index, which for decades has seemingly predicted world events. The idea is to monitor DC-area pizza shops for spikes in business, the thinking being that a crisis would require late nights for those working in national security, and they would need to order something to eat.](https://substack.com/redirect/1c887da7-a308-470c-95d8-69518069fc9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/1c887da7-a308-470c-95d8-69518069fc9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Even if we don't know _how_ it is going to end, we know that it certainly _will_. And whatever that end-state is, Iran will not be the threat that it once was. But there are lots of unintended consequences. It's worth reflecting on the reality that the current glue holding together the Middle East is hatred and fear of the Iranians. Indeed, the insight that Sunni Arab countries were more worried about Iran than Israel led to the Abraham Accords--bilateral agreements in which several countries normalized relations with Israel. The Accords were fundamentally an anti-Iran project and were seen as such by the Iranian regime.
Iran targeting Dubai and other Arab nations last week cemented the Sunni-Israel alignment (at least for the short term.) But _if the Iranian regime falls, what holds the coalition together?_ Do the Gulf states revert to being primarily concerned with Israel and the Palestinian question? The common enemy was doing a lot of structural work that nobody had to say out loud. Remove it, and we'll find out how durable these relationships really are.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/134ad1db-8726-40de-a228-75ea3ddc4c95?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The military operation will probably succeed. It already is well on its way. But if Iraq taught us anything, it's that **the war is the easy part.** What comes after--re-establishing governance, restoring social services, and retaining know-how and capability--that's the hard part. That's when the gains from military success are solidified and the people of Iran can be shown a path towards a better future.
So I ask again: How will this end?
| | POLL
---
How will this end? Iran will be a...
| [democracy with booming economy](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.a2Gx3jDZeW5s_pMa2GJhg-BpHvOXtLeJxxL08ZyRz-c?)
---
[failed state; no basic governance](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.hg-cWrEW8LfN6EVA3Bj9Wlf11AX4UaQbk7DzoAu5ylY?)
[harsh Islamic state (no weapons)](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.dr4VYS_f-5qbfviCx5YL6VHQJT0qSEbNRt12wCTXj9g?)
[Chinese colony loyal to Beijing](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.20_awSnxlG4da_X4bv_9Jy-ByEh8y-W0OD7e4ki8b1U?)

## **My log of Iranian attacks:**
In addition to Israel, it has also attacked:
* [UAE](https://substack.com/redirect/e72adfbd-50d7-4d26-984f-b5cbda017f9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Iran launched 941 drones, 189 ballistic missiles and 8 cruise missiles at the UAE in the first week alone. A drone struck the Burj Al Arab hotel in Dubai. Abu Dhabi's airport was shut down. A fire broke out near the US consulate in Dubai. The Fujairah Oil Terminal was hit by falling interception debris.
* [Saudi Arabia](https://substack.com/redirect/1fa35adc-ad21-4c53-85c1-fcac41b3caac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Two Iranian drones targeted the US embassy in Riyadh, causing a fire. Missiles were intercepted near Riyadh's airport and Prince Sultan Air Base. A drone strike forced Saudi Aramco to shut down the Ras Tanura refinery -- the kingdom's largest, processing 550,000 barrels per day. It was hit a second time two days later.
* [Qatar](https://substack.com/redirect/c528a5eb-3f0e-437a-9e12-bb5f66dc93d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Air defenses intercepted at least two waves of missile attacks over Doha. Iranian drones struck a QatarEnergy facility at Ras Laffan, the world's largest LNG producer, forcing a halt in production. A second drone targeted a water tank at a power plant in Mesaieed. Qatar arrested 10 suspected IRGC spies.
* [Kuwait](https://substack.com/redirect/457fc9ca-e007-4ce3-9b6b-70b8d8b23c75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Kuwait International Airport was struck. The US embassy was hit and closed indefinitely. Three US fighter jets were accidentally shot down by Kuwaiti air defenses in the chaos.
* [Bahrain](https://substack.com/redirect/cd737374-d3d9-4e5a-a54b-3ddcc1b9ebd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- The US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Juffair was targeted multiple times. Bahrain's international airport was hit by a drone. Residential buildings in Manama were struck. One worker was killed by debris from an intercepted missile.
* [Jordan](https://substack.com/redirect/a0ee7e64-ef62-4c0b-85e6-b239a7b57303?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Jordanian armed forces reported intercepting 49 drones and ballistic missiles. Reports confirmed missiles hit Jordanian territory including areas near Amman.
* [Iraq](https://substack.com/redirect/a0ee7e64-ef62-4c0b-85e6-b239a7b57303?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Iranian aerial attacks targeted infrastructure hosting US military and civilian personnel, including Erbil International Airport and the US Consulate in Erbil. A drone targeted a military base with US assets near Baghdad International Airport. The IRGC separately struck Kurdish opposition positions in western Iraq with dozens of drones.
* [Oman](https://substack.com/redirect/cd737374-d3d9-4e5a-a54b-3ddcc1b9ebd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Two drones hit Duqm port, injuring one foreign worker. An oil tanker was attacked about 5 nautical miles off Oman's Musandam coast, injuring four people.
* [Azerbaijan](https://substack.com/redirect/fc2f2786-5f6d-468b-a7a9-ad346e30716f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Iranian drones hit the airport in Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan exclave.
* [Cyprus (UK base)](https://substack.com/redirect/f6053f62-877c-4410-bd8e-c52dcc299090?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Britain's Akrotiri and Dhekelia military base was struck by Iranian kamikaze drones -- drawing a European country into the conflict for the first time.
* [Lebanon](https://substack.com/redirect/f2be63ec-c975-4ffd-977d-3ce8b616e51e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- Hezbollah entered the war by launching rockets at northern Israel and Israel responded with massive strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs and the Bekaa Valley. More than 217 killed in Lebanon since the war began.
* [At sea](https://substack.com/redirect/f2be63ec-c975-4ffd-977d-3ce8b616e51e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- The US-flagged tanker _Stena Imperative_ and the Honduras-flagged _Athe Nova_ were struck. A senior IRGC official vowed to "set fire to any ship coming through the Strait of Hormuz." The UN's International Maritime Organization reported 20,000 seafarers and 15,000 cruise passengers stranded.

**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/17442a7e-408a-4b6f-8489-5f24ba2eff67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/298325b5-f072-4bf6-8b33-379263a3392f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/671c976a-7f01-44c3-b3d9-f464761a589b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

Winning the war in Iran may be easy; securing the peace will be more challenging. | | | Mar 8 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- In March 2003, as the 101st Airborne rolled toward Baghdad, its commander--a two-star general named David Petraeus--turned to an embedded and asked for a glimpse into the future: "Tell me how this ends." It became the most famous quote of the Iraq War, and eerily prescient. We invaded, toppled Saddam, disbanded the army, purged the government, and then spent twenty years (and a few trillion dollars) discovering that winning the war was the easy part. And now, as the United States and Israel decisively move to put the final nails in the Iranian regime's coffin, I can't help but ask: "Are we destined to make the same mistakes we did last time?" On the surface, it seems we've learned a great deal. We aren't pouring in troops or dumping in billions of dollars, and U.S. President Donald Trump doesn't appear to have much appetite for nation-building or democracy-promotion. He even seems reluctant when it comes to regime change, instead suggesting the people of Iran should take on that project. And yet, I am now asking myself the same question Petraeus asked 20 years ago: How is this going to end? So far, the military campaign seems to be progressing well, but it doesn't seem like much thought has been given to what follows. As former State Department official Gabe Noronha noted earlier this year, the United States is great at removing hostile leaders, but we're not as good at setting up the country for post-conflict governance. It's been one week since the U.S. and Israel . Khamenei is dead. Tehran is burning. . Six American service members are gone. Already it appears that the rest of the region is being drawn into the conflict. In its last dying gasp, the regime has lashed out and attacked 11 countries and international shipping. (See the bottom of this post for my summary of the regional attacks). These attacks have already spurred some of these countries to join in on the effort. If this conflict continues to spread - dragging in more countries, the EU, Turkey - the impact could be widespread and destabilizing. Remember the unintended consequences of the Arab Spring? The Muslim Brotherhood in charge of Egypt (for a time), Libya and Syria disintegrating into anarchy, a wave of refugees flooding Europe and spurring a populist backlash...the disintegration of Iran could be materially more problematic for the region. And let's not forget about the context in which this is happening... a global economy that's been battling inflation, coping with artificial intelligence disruption, trade and tariff wars, and currency confusion. In the midst of those cross-currents, Iran's strategy-- - is de facto shutting down the Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint through which 20% or so of global oil travels. | | ---|---|--- , , and the Dow is dropping like a rock. It would be silly to suggest that this impact is not being contemplated by Republican and Democrat strategists developing their messaging for the 2026 midterm elections. Remember the ? If this drags into the summer, what is the impact going to be on the economy? Could elevated commodity prices drive inflation higher, leading the Fed to adopt a "wait and see" strategy rather than lowering rates? What would the impact of such a decision be on the US housing market? Is it conceivable we'll be facing a stagnant or contracting economy and inflation, in a repeat of the 1970s oil-shock driven stagflation? The Iranian regime has been and much of its leadership has been killed. Moreover, the intense bombing is taking out not only military targets but also intelligence and security sites. Who is going to be left to secure the country? Or run the country? This is the kinetic version of the de-Baathification that barred anyone with ties to Saddam's Baath Party from government, including from the military and police. Overnight, the people who knew how to keep the peace, keep the lights on, run the courts, manage the water supply, were all gone. What followed was anarchy, then insurgency, then civil war. Is Iran headed down a similar path? Let's take a closer look. The IRGC isn't just a military force. It runs construction companies, banks, telecom networks, and import-export businesses. It is a state within a state. When you decapitate the Supreme Leader, kill key commanders, and destroy the IRGC's infrastructure, you haven't just removed a regime. You've removed the operating system of the country. Who is going to reboot what's left? Look at Libya and Syria to see how this could unfold. Indeed, who is going to run the country after the war ends is very much an open question. The is on the sideline, chomping at the bit to get into the game, but it is not clear how much support he has inside the country. One thing is clear: he certainly doesn't have an army. How he or anyone else would secure the country still very much remains to be seen. This reminds me very much of , the Iraqi exile who was supposed to run a pro-Western, U.S.-back government after the war. Didn't exactly work out as hoped. and has called Mojtaba Khamenei "unacceptable." But the question isn't who leads Iran next. It's who runs the electricity grid, staffs the hospitals, and prevents the country from fracturing into armed fiefdoms. We didn't have that answer in Iraq. We don't have it in Iran. Any attempt to secure the country is going to involve boots on the ground, but it's clear that the administration doesn't want American feet in those boots. That's why the CIA is reportedly , with . But is it a good idea to use a minority group to overthrow an ethnic majority? Indeed, this isn't the first time we've . There was the first Iraq war, then the second, then Syria, to name a few. And after the war ends, the Kurds tend to be reluctant to give up their arms; just look at what's now. Let's not forget that Iran is a multi-ethnic country; could this end with its balkanization? This move sure seems like a glidepath to civil war, similar to Iraq. Plus anything involving the Kurds and guns always sets their neighbors on edge. and with its finger on the trigger. It certainly hasn't hesitated to . | | | | ---|---|--- | June 29, 2025 | Even if we don't know how it is going to end, we know that it certainly will. And whatever that end-state is, Iran will not be the threat that it once was. But there are lots of unintended consequences. It's worth reflecting on the reality that the current glue holding together the Middle East is hatred and fear of the Iranians. Indeed, the insight that Sunni Arab countries were more worried about Iran than Israel led to the Abraham Accords--bilateral agreements in which several countries normalized relations with Israel. The Accords were fundamentally an anti-Iran project and were seen as such by the Iranian regime. Iran targeting Dubai and other Arab nations last week cemented the Sunni-Israel alignment (at least for the short term.) But if the Iranian regime falls, what holds the coalition together? Do the Gulf states revert to being primarily concerned with Israel and the Palestinian question? The common enemy was doing a lot of structural work that nobody had to say out loud. Remove it, and we'll find out how durable these relationships really are. The military operation will probably succeed. It already is well on its way. But if Iraq taught us anything, it's that the war is the easy part. What comes after--re-establishing governance, restoring social services, and retaining know-how and capability--that's the hard part. That's when the gains from military success are solidified and the people of Iran can be shown a path towards a better future. So I ask again: How will this end? | | POLL How will this end? Iran will be a... | ? ? ? ? ? Friend, it's Women's History Month, and I've been reflecting on the experiences that have brought me to where I am today.
Back in the day, I never imagined I’d run for the U.S. Senate – let alone become just the third Jewish woman and first former synagogue president ever _elected_ to the Senate.

Long before I ever set foot in Congress, I was a cocktail waitress working hard to pay the bills and become the first in my family to graduate from college. I worked as a computer programmer and systems analyst in what has long been a male-dominated field. During that time in my life, I saw firsthand the pay discrimination and challenges that come for women in the workplace.
But it's _these_ experiences that have driven so much of my work in the Senate. They're why I fight so hard to inspire young girls to pursue careers in STEM and ensure women have a seat at the leadership table where decisions are made. They're why I fight so hard for paycheck fairness, for affordable child care, and for reproductive freedom.
It's also why I have a wall in my Senate office that's filled with photos of fierce, dedicated, trailblazing women who have paved the way for me and fought for our rights throughout history. They remind me every day of what we can collectively achieve when we set our goals high – and that this fight for _full_ equality is far from over.
**My experiences will always guide my fight to make positive change for communities in Nevada and across the country. But right now, I’m counting on the grassroots support of people like you to power this fight in the Senate.**
_[**So please, in honor of Women's History Month, will you make a gift of $5 or more now to help Democrats take back the Senate, power our fight**** against hate and discrimination, and secure true equality?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126905375/606333539/-105825184?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5720-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OTZmMjhjYi1mNzFhLWYxMTEtYTY5YS0wMDBkM2ExZjBhOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ckLY1T_EZz5gcoTtf-39UoBUTHIwrQxh0_D9uW3L5r8=)_

clean_body: | | | = Friend, it's Women's History Month, and I've been reflecting on the experiences that have brought me to where I am today. Back in the day, I never imagined I’d run for the U.S. Senate – let alone become just the third Jewish woman and first former synagogue president ever elected to the Senate. Long before I ever set foot in Congress, I was a cocktail waitress working hard to pay the bills and become the first in my family to graduate from college. I worked as a computer programmer and systems analyst in what has long been a male-dominated field. During that time in my life, I saw firsthand the pay discrimination and challenges that come for women in the workplace. But it's these experiences that have driven so much of my work in the Senate. They're why I fight so hard to inspire young girls to pursue careers in STEM and ensure women have a seat at the leadership table where decisions are made. They're why I fight so hard for paycheck fairness, for affordable child care, and for reproductive freedom. It's also why I have a wall in my Senate office that's filled with photos of fierce, dedicated, trailblazing women who have paved the way for me and fought for our rights throughout history. They remind me every day of what we can collectively achieve when we set our goals high – and that this fight for full equality is far from over. My experiences will always guide my fight to make positive change for communities in Nevada and across the country. But right now, I’m counting on the grassroots support of people like you to power this fight in the Senate. = | | = Thank you. Jacky | | = A note from Sen. Jacky Rosen: Friend, I’m fighting tirelessly in the Senate to deliver progress for people across Nevada and our country. But with Donald Trump in the White House and Republicans in control of both houses of Congress, I need your grassroots support to keep up the fights that matter most. Senate Democrats like me are the only thing standing in the way of Trump's extreme agenda that will hurt everyday Americans. That's why I need people like you on my side to help defend our progress and take back power from the GOP. = You can also =** to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive updates from my campaign. name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
Hey, it’s Mark Kelly.
Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.
I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.
But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.
So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.
I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the full weight of the executive branch bearing down on me right now.
After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.
**[I’m asking you to split a donation between Chris Pappas and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260308_1._icO9V&amount=5&refcode=em20260308-1&t=1&akid=5451%2E582294%2EAyUD-3)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260308_1._icO9V&amount=5&refcode=em20260308-1&t=20&akid=5451%2E582294%2EAyUD-3)**
Thank you.
Mark
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260308_1._icO9V&amount=5&refcode=em20260308-1&t=21&akid=5451%2E582294%2EAyUD-3)
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20260308-1&t=22&refcode2=5451_582294_AyUD-3&akid=5451%2E582294%2EAyUD-3)**

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Happy International Women's Day, friend!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6110?t=1&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)
---
Happy International Women's Day, friend!

Today, I’m thinking about my great-grandmother, mother, and many before them. My mother was born in a train boxcar where the Laguna people built a community far from home. She served in the U.S. Navy, raised four kids as a military wife, and spent more than two decades in Indian education.

I've been blessed to come from generations of resilient and fierce Pueblo women. They taught me to Be Fierce — to protect our communities and never back down.
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6082?t=2&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)
And that’s exactly what I’ve done. I’ve stood up to corporate polluters. I rebuilt the Department of the Interior after Trump slashed staff, cut budgets, and rolled back protections. I defended our public lands and our people when they were under attack.

And I won’t stop now.
Friend, while Trump and his allies are doing everything to tear us down: Gutting health care. Raising costs. Ignoring the affordability crisis. Empowering ICE to terrorize families and sow fear in our neighborhoods. Dismantling our voting rights. Attacking reproductive freedom and dividing us to hold onto power. **_Destroying our very democracy._**
I’m fighting back.

I was a single mom. I relied on Medicaid, WIC, and SNAP. I worked multiple jobs and started a small salsa business to get by. I know how to keep fighting even when it feels like it isn’t getting any easier — and Trump definitely isn’t making it any easier.

That’s why I’m prepared to lead.
I understand affordability because I’ve lived it. I know how critical strong public schools, accessible healthcare, and safe communities are because my family relied on them.

And I’ve led at the highest levels of government — in Congress and as U.S. Secretary of the Interior — where I took on tough fights and delivered real results.

**_Experience matters. Leadership matters. And I’m ready to be the governor for all New Mexicans._**
But at a time when women are being sidelined by the Trump administration, we must work even harder to protect, uplift, and stand together. **I will always be a champion for women's rights and fight for our communities, friend, but I can't do it without you.[In honor of International Women's Day, would you consider making a contribution of $5 to my campaign for governor? Together, we will build a future that honors women and where everyone can thrive.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6111?t=3&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6112?t=4&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)
---
| | [Donate $35 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6113?t=5&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)
---
| | [Donate $50 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6114?t=6&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)
---
| | [Other Amount >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/6082?t=7&akid=8035%2E400544%2E6ZrIDL)
---
Stay fierce,

Deb
|
* * *

---

They might even remove him from office. Our country is hanging in the balance, and when you become a Sustaining Member, YOU become our first line of defense against the socialist Democrats. Plus, you 'll be working directly with me to KEEP MAKING AMERICA GREAT! **** I look forward to working together, Donald Trump Jr. Paid for by the Republican National Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Navy SEAL Adam Schwarze
subject: My Democrat opponent regrets protecting families.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
Chip in $2 today to send a SEAL who stands firm—no regrets. | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c70b96bef35e4a26a62bcd65068523b4&_e=C3hNAcqhZFG2pth13LHdEoN33Q--YnHlL6qdQHZ1itAgbJ9JKLbTKCh9oVkKIuHPAHd5hhAbA3ch3BG2pqNqdze2TTdbSFf1oPdwg4qHIRRl17xmJKmj9FpIxfUvr_Lv5CCx80slK7uS-6heAG_q7enb6WKTDEEfhQbX7MbopDGACJYeAOcJRuTSmUo80aeywh7V72LR9lfjUI4QKXJijFi1tP8ww54xvIx5nv5jvJEc01cGVwgMywGfao_DNNHuuAczDyPXdJ6w-V1N6VlOEG7y1wy2v7HWSGaXD6UUezIhZytrbRNOnlD7-yOiU4HCCOMZy6UqKwIDO6Lwb7YCHWU29hQom23UbdicA69tS7mTkQJTWfBmE1YR7tjeCJPVaHQkge34CQB0SXyR2qQZF8kPwHSRiaMEiBVEE-2FKv-XMi_24KhfsQa_akd-09F_RQqoY3G4LzBil7pc-_vkOZ3EyzEjccNz3PV_mIIq3gR4JYoVW_1DXMMZPwGOnIxk2CdmfpIV92k10BERNKwsEyet-rw_qxIKA3WIEHwcTlohW-HcX-UwFkL91copUX8zxWHpVS_n-q2UlwbxUFAEZHnaZ7gMnz9_d0CUehyrSnVKEu_y0wPjXYZbCVxEk6AtGZU3xYRjRwtrVjXe4MvkiDHAKyXpelz3hVOiwz7ypXcBxPreochsDwgnKqalH6CRcgXAt01yZDY5terq_ZhJXvSu1JyPxDlELSGkClzn9o5LW16Q1Bpk8S3SA3jAdcUz)

---
Peter,

I'm Adam Schwarze, current Republican U.S. Senate candidate in Minnesota, a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and a Minnesotan who deployed nine times to keep threats far from our shores—so families like yours could live without fear.

As a SEAL, I learned early: You don't second-guess the mission once it's underway. You commit, you execute, you finish—no retreat, no regrets.

That's why Rep. Angie Craig's (a Democrat opponent in the race) latest flip-flop hits so hard.

_**Angie voted for the Laken Riley Act—a Trump-backed, common-sense bill to detain dangerous illegal immigrants and honor victims like Laken Riley, a young woman murdered by someone who never should have been here in the first place.**_

It was the right call for safer communities. But now, in her own words from her recent Star Tribune op-ed: **"I regret my vote."**

_**Does she regret standing up for the victims of violent crime?**_****

_**Does she regret supporting detention for criminals who prey on Americans?**_

She flipped after pressure from radicals and amid Trump's enforcement push—choosing politics over protection.

_**From my viewpoint, that's not leadership.**_

And Peggy Flanagan? She's been attacking enforcement all along, never even pretending to prioritize safety.

[**help me flip minnesota republican
**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c70b96bef35e4a26a62bcd65068523b4&_e=C3hNAcqhZFG2pth13LHdEoN33Q--YnHlL6qdQHZ1itAgbJ9JKLbTKCh9oVkKIuHPAHd5hhAbA3ch3BG2pqNqdze2TTdbSFf1oPdwg4qHIRRl17xmJKmj9FpIxfUvr_Lv5CCx80slK7uS-6heAG_q7enb6WKTDEEfhQbX7MbopDGACJYeAOcJRuTSmUo80aeywh7V72LR9lfjUI4QKXJijFi1tP8ww54xvIx5nv5jvJEc01cGVwgMywGfao_DNNHuuAczDyPXdJ6w-V1N6VlOENeDpA5coRzg04SsubCNWiE7aPcvhyc3ujlnOa5n5Qt_SzuE6W7v-4NGQMrgta6oJdEBizEuydcuTn2QI_AyO2B3RDWPq_UWZyGGoqCdsNyYeUCkappWwsFuQDRaYE0Rp9PqoEE3LQCH9PZVxNIdwSDeAxOY6dGTU2jD6FPWiNvoijuz2X8kYQ_tOqBIqEtUBqcYv2GMRq5p9KKDfIj8GB7euSD7DEiITChPY0MhPI1Eok3uknIKEiwb-dKzGRs98sPB8n5P2tb3rzTEKhuXv9SrcgozbJOD_To5Hl7SNLBkmz7JdQMmuXtAF_Zc5ELEPQSuFDr725bhxBQcNjJFOw96msg5eKOYMNZ87kcMCiTgSbaE4WFGLj_iuPNyiPqYfKELl0koYbsKTDJZ-iuNMyBNmMfNT6Ms_ArNhTwMieVOK3tLCXfJKll4DNJHRl6i8SjW6xko6keJT9eK5WJAufTyY4np35SWYLZZ-hTWRmU1)
Minnesota families deserve better. Your kids and grandkids deserve neighborhoods where they can play outside again—like I did growing up in the '80s and 90s—without constant fear of crime fueled by open borders and weak policies.

**Those safer days shouldn't be a distant memory.** They can be our reality if we send reinforcements to back President Trump's agenda in the United States Senate: strong borders, backing the blue, zero tolerance for chaos.

President Trump needs fighters in the Senate who won't waver when the heat is on. He already has one Navy SEAL proving what real resolve looks like—Sen. Tim Sheehy. Help me join him and double the SEAL population.

**[Chip in just $2 today](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c70b96bef35e4a26a62bcd65068523b4&_e=C3hNAcqhZFG2pth13LHdEoN33Q--YnHlL6qdQHZ1itAgbJ9JKLbTKCh9oVkKIuHPAHd5hhAbA3ch3BG2pqNqdze2TTdbSFf1oPdwg4qHIRRl17xmJKmj9FpIxfUvr_Lv5CCx80slK7uS-6heAG_q7enb6WKTDEEfhQbX7MbopDGACJYeAOcJRuTSmUo80aeywh7V72LR9lfjUI4QKXJijFi1tP8ww54xvIx5nv5jvJEc01cGVwgMywGfao_DNNHuuAczDyPXdJ6w-V1N6VlOEL7o9YtgOxuVjIHIdr7Nw8juWIz49hmajyl8FzoasRL8yGwe1dwp8LMdGgM90DD29J-LCr8GWnVtaRhH61G2Yppv5FoImGwQlE0xCpJmSDI2thI3Xjzjo8GB0GZ0COHbiY-XCZgA8Jli1p6z3yYZ8nML6dx1pyg6gIknO6RbiXJL-NsXWkVLkMWs4RifMCosnlVDxHE4xFFhokCUbsYmKKy6pdXPzPNMpWm7g6S8pWksBWxPoaUPBVCdfmw8sv2CQFhYwTfpOuXvS3TdghV9-pcrbgfkPTaqtISYFNTz21f0b1FKVPZfW5HEQBIW8kWJWH41J3OhM0VF2vO0_z5OJKdaueTSXSYvkspeh_tbAC6SLTALB28lM2RJ3nzRzdBC5Zj4rUPG3k47KPM17b-rvXizHT0GYHmp0Xe2tdiKO3QOzIiWoshSlyRLh_B77ucZ2pWbMKZej3kpoFbrf7CnS80LitFjajC3Rd38BrZqDK1c)** —no more, no less if that's what you can do—to stand with a veteran who finishes what he starts.

I grew up working-class, learned duty from my parents, joined the Marines right after 9/11 on my 18th birthday, and then became a SEAL. Nine deployments across 70+ countries, always carrying my mom's and dad's prayers for a safe return.

Now I'm asking the people of Minnesota to deploy me again – to protect families from flip-flopping politicians who regret doing the right thing when it counts.

This won't be easy. Democrats have the money and the machine. But close races are won one dollar, one voice at a time. Remember 2008? **The Minnesota Senate race was decided by 312 votes. **

clean_body: Chip in $2 today to send a SEAL who stands firm—no regrets. | Peter, I'm Adam Schwarze, current Republican U.S. Senate candidate in Minnesota, a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and a Minnesotan who deployed nine times to keep threats far from our shores—so families like yours could live without fear. As a SEAL, I learned early: You don't second-guess the mission once it's underway. You commit, you execute, you finish—no retreat, no regrets. That's why Rep. Angie Craig's (a Democrat opponent in the race) latest flip-flop hits so hard. Angie voted for the Laken Riley Act—a Trump-backed, common-sense bill to detain dangerous illegal immigrants and honor victims like Laken Riley, a young woman murdered by someone who never should have been here in the first place. It was the right call for safer communities. But now, in her own words from her recent Star Tribune op-ed: "I regret my vote." Does she regret standing up for the victims of violent crime?** Does she regret supporting detention for criminals who prey on Americans? She flipped after pressure from radicals and amid Trump's enforcement push—choosing politics over protection. From my viewpoint, that's not leadership. And Peggy Flanagan? She's been attacking enforcement all along, never even pretending to prioritize safety. Minnesota families deserve better. Your kids and grandkids deserve neighborhoods where they can play outside again—like I did growing up in the '80s and 90s—without constant fear of crime fueled by open borders and weak policies. Those safer days shouldn't be a distant memory. They can be our reality if we send reinforcements to back President Trump's agenda in the United States Senate: strong borders, backing the blue, zero tolerance for chaos. President Trump needs fighters in the Senate who won't waver when the heat is on. He already has one Navy SEAL proving what real resolve looks like—Sen. Tim Sheehy. Help me join him and double the SEAL population. —no more, no less if that's what you can do—to stand with a veteran who finishes what he starts. I grew up working-class, learned duty from my parents, joined the Marines right after 9/11 on my 18th birthday, and then became a SEAL. Nine deployments across 70+ countries, always carrying my mom's and dad's prayers for a safe return. Now I'm asking the people of Minnesota to deploy me again – to protect families from flip-flopping politicians who regret doing the right thing when it counts. This won't be easy. Democrats have the money and the machine. But close races are won one dollar, one voice at a time. Remember 2008? The Minnesota Senate race was decided by 312 votes. If you're a parent or grandparent who knows what it means to guard what matters most, Help send a message: No regrets, no flip-flops—just strength. I'm a fighter—a son, a veteran, a man ready to defend American families without apology. For love of country, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA** | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Just checking in before midnight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Roy Cooper for North Carolina
body:
I wanted to make sure you had a chance to take our new priorities survey
| | | | |
---
Peter,
I wanted to make sure you had a chance to take our new priorities survey before we close submissions at midnight. This is a grassroots campaign – powered by and for the people – so it’s important I hear from you directly. That’s how I’ll do my job and ensure I’m fighting for the things you care about most.
So please, I would love to hear from you. This is the last call. **Will you share your thoughts with me in this survey?**
Here’s the best link to respond: **_[surveys.signforgood.com/cooper-priorities](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6948?email=&zip=&t=6&akid=712%2E124072%2E1cV1Pe)_**
Thank you,

Roy
| |
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6533?source=&t=1&akid=712%2E124072%2E1cV1Pe)**

---
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=5&akid=712%2E124072%2E1cV1Pe)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Roy Cooper for North Carolina
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=712%2E124072%2E1cV1Pe)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=7&akid=712%2E124072%2E1cV1Pe)

name: The Democratic Party
subject: An explanation →
body:
---
Derek, can we have a moment to explain what’s happening right now? Here’s the deal:
* Trump has threatened to “take over” the midterm elections and continued to push conspiracy theories of voter fraud.
* He’s deployed masked federal agents to blue states.
* He even accused Democratic officials of “seditious behavior” for urging military and intelligence personnel not to obey unlawful orders and suggested they should be executed for it.

Derek, Trump wants people to be afraid. He wants to intimidate us into silence. He and his allies are counting on Democrats completely giving up. We can’t let that happen. At the end of this month, we face our next end-of-quarter fundraising deadline. After this deadline passes, we’ll be required to report what we’ve raised. Those numbers will tell a story: A lot of contributions will show the strength of Democrats’ opposition to Trump and his agenda. _They’ll show we aren’t done fighting._ [**So, Derek, we’re asking: Can you join thousands of your fellow Democrats in making a contribution to the DNC today? It will add up fast when we all come together to do our part:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?amount=7&refcode=em_20260308_6315_fr&refcode2=6315_22930333_bmXAsz&akid=6315%2E22930333%2EbmXAsz) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260308_6315_fr&refcode2=6315_22930333_bmXAsz&akid=6315%2E22930333%2EbmXAsz)**

subject: Happy International Women's Day!

body:
| | | Friend, today I am excited to celebrate International Women's Day.

As Chair of the DSCC, it's my mission to elect Democrats who will make historic progress on issues like reproductive freedom, lowering household costs, and ending gender discrimination ‐‐ **[but in order to do so, we need resources. Please, will you chip in $5 now to help elect Democrats to the Senate who will fight for our rights?>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/22350?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260308-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandIWD2026-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260308-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandIWD2026-Pros-sl1&akid=23015%2E9716520%2Enj7YB_)**

I am proud to have been a champion for women in the Senate and beyond, from fighting for reproductive health care to working to expand protections for domestic laborers.

I hope to continue this work not only by emphasizing these crucial issues on my own, but also by helping elect even more Democrats to the Senate who will fight for progress alongside me. In fact, I'm proud to be part of a Democratic caucus that includes a near‐record number of Democratic women -- and if things go well this November, I'm confident _we can blow that record out of the water._

Across the map, we have amazing Democrats running in battleground races -- _every one of whom_ I know will be in that fight alongside me. **So as DSCC Chair, I'm personally asking:**

**[Please, this International Women's Day, join me in the fight by chipping in $5, or whatever you can spare, to help elect more Democrats who will fight for women!](https://go.dscc.org/go/22350?amount=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260308-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandIWD2026-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260308-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandIWD2026-Pros-sl1&akid=23015%2E9716520%2Enj7YB_)**
